Based on the image provided, it appears that the patient has a significant amount of retinal fluid, which is indicative of macular edema, a common complication of PCV (polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy). The presence of retinal fluid suggests that the patient may benefit from continued anti-VEGF injections to help reduce the fluid and prevent further damage to the retina.

Anti-VEGF injections are a common treatment for macular edema associated with PCV, as they help to reduce the amount of fluid in the retina by inhibiting the production of vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), a protein that promotes the growth of new blood vessels and can lead to fluid accumulation in the retina.

Therefore, based on the image, it is likely that the patient will need to continue with anti-VEGF injections to manage their macular edema and prevent further vision
